---
title: "Mary Elizabeth Latimer"
---

## Mary Elizabeth Latimer

**Dates:** (1822-1904)

**Maiden name:** (Wormeley)

### Description

American historian, contributor to <i>MP</i> (1879-93)

### Biography

She lived in Baltimore, Maryland, and published two novels as Elizabeth Wormeley: <i>Amabel: A Family History</i> (1853) and <i>Our Cousin Veronica, or, Scenes and Adventures Over the Blue Ridge</i> (1855). In the 1880s she began writing again, publishing three novels under the name Elizabeth Wormeley Latimer:  <i>Salvage</i> (1880), <i>My Wife and My Wife\'s Sister</i>, 1881, and <i>Princess Amélie: A Fragment of Autobiography</i> (1883). In 1888 she published three translations from the French: <i>History of the People of Israel</i>, by Ernest Renan, and <i>For Fifteen Years</i> and <i>Steel Hammer</i>, both  by Louis Ulbach and in 1890 George Sand’s <i>Nanon</i>. In the 1890s and early 1900s she published a series of books on the history of various European nations, and on the colonisation of Africa, three more translations and a final novel,  <i>Prince Incognito</i> (1902).

### Letters

- Letter from Mar 5th 1896
- Letter from April 1st 1890[[footnote:1]]
- Letter from Sept 20th 1889
- Letter from Feb 10th 1888
- Letter from June 1st [1886?]

---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
